title: Concerning The Right To Disconnect
summary: SB-5192 concerns the Right to Disconnect, a labor policy ensuring that workers reserve the right to refuse nonessential communication from their employers outside of working hours.

title: Concerning The Establishment of a Public Works Administration
summary: HB-003 concerns the establishment of a Public Works Administration in Washington to receive five billion dollars annually from the general fund. The Public Works Administration will hire Washingtonians to assist in the development of various projects across Washington, primarily related to infrastructure, as authorized by the Governor via order. HB-003 stipulates that Public Works Administration employees shall be paid a minimum of $14/hour and shall be subject to the same labor rights regulations as any other employee within Washington State.

title: Concerning The Adoption of an Opt-Out Public Healthcare Option
summary:
HB-002 concerns the adoption of an opt-out public healthcare option in Washington State, colloquially dubbed "MontgomeryCare". The bill aims to establish an opt-out public healthcare option, cap prescription drug costs to a bracketed dividend ranked by severity of medication (low-risk, life-saving, etc), establish a maximum claim denial rate on healthcare insurance providers, incentivize the employment and education of medical professionals, and require that dental, hearing, and vision coverage be provided in healthcare insurance packages granted by employers, among other purposes. 

(A federal rendition of the bill text can be found here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ummoi5tSuoQZrkAG8zbhmrlXfXqorh8XULt36VmCIoI/edit?usp=sharing. For RP purposes, the sections of this legislation have been retrofitted to fit Washington State.)

MontgomeryCare is funded via the MontgomeryCare trust fund, which is considered to be the special account fund for the Public Health Insurance Corporation.

title: Concerning A Ban On Capital Punishment
summary: HB-0001 allows for a referendum to take place in the 2020 general election, providing for the ban of capital punishment in the Washington Constitution. The exact text of the amendment reads: "No court of competent jurisdiction shall render death as a punishment for any crime committed within the jurisdiction of Washington state, and any law, regulation, statute, or otherwise codified item of law in the state of Washington providing for the penalty of death as a result of a crime is considered struck."
